title: Automated Quantification of Dopaminergic Neurons in Floating Sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tvrdf","text":"Standardized procedure for automated detection and quantification of dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra pars compacta (SNc) and ventral tegmental area (VTA) in floating brain sections using image acquisition, AI-based segmentation, and downstream data analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7o1bv","text":"An GA3 macro in NIS-Elements (Nikon imaging software) is an analysis script that automates image processing and quantitative measurements. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5it3u","text":"All GA3 macros necessary for this protocol are available here: https://github.com/Louis-EricTrudeau/Trudeau-lab/tree/main/Mukherjee-2026/GA3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":63,"length":77}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"EEF6A0A943E811F19A3B0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://github.com/Louis-EricTrudeau/Trudeau-lab/tree/main/Mukherjee-2026/GA3"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"hmofh","text":"- Slide scanner (Zeiss AxioScan 7)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5itcv","text":"- Analysis Workstation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kh8b2","text":"- Zen software (Zeiss)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qwqr","text":"- NIS-Elements Advance Research software (Nikon) with GA3 pipelines Jobs (https://github.com/Louis-EricTrudeau/Trudeau-lab/tree/main/Mukherjee-2026/GA3), and Batch Deconvolution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":74,"length":77}],"data":{}},{"key":"ixwav","text":"- GraphPad Prism","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ib6fw","text":"- Brain atlas and TH atlas (see step 11)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"F81CF81543E811F1BEA10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://github.com/Louis-EricTrudeau/Trudeau-lab/tree/main/Mukherjee-2026/GA3"}}}}
steps: Scan regions of interest with a slide scanner.
Delineate the SNc and VTA regions (containing DA neurons) using GA3 "7-Draw SNc and VTA".
Use standard atlas and TH Atlas as references: atlas TH with SNc and VTA traced.pdf
Open each image manually.
Assign rostro-caudal coordinates relative to bregma in the Excel output file.
Transfer data to AUC calculation sheet.
Verify segmentation accuracy visually after AI processing. Confirm correct anatomical delineation using atlas references.
Define one region of interest (ROI) per hemisphere (Figure 1) from pre-scan at 5x. Include all sections containing RFP-positive neurons. Capture reference screenshots of selected regions. Figure 1
Acquire images of defined ROIs at 20x in z-stacks at 1µm interval using the following channels: DAPI, TH, NeuN, and RFP. Use parameters shown in Figure 2 and 3. Figure 2 Figure 3
Convert images to TIFF format in batch using Zen. Processing - Batch Batch Method: OME TIFF-Export Method Parameters: see Figure 4 Figure 4
Apply GA3 “6-Run AI-2 on TIFF” to all images in batch mode.

Cut the mouse brain coronally at 40µm thickness and collect one 40 µm coronal section out of every three for immunohistochemistry. Perform an immunohistochemistry labeling for DAPI, TH, NeuN, and RFP (for a genetic mouse model expressing tdTomato in dopamine neurons).
Apply GA3 “2-Export with 1 channel” on NIS-Elements software to extract the RFP channel.
Perform FAST deconvolution on 4 representative images using NIS-Elements software.
Generate segmentation masks using the GA3 "3-Pre-AI-1 macro on decon": - Adjust BrightSpots (grow) and FilterObjects boxes - Save images with the segmentation masks.
Apply AI-1 to 7 additional images using GA3 “4-Run AI-1 on samples TIFF”.
AI-1 Training:
AI-1 Application:
Train AI-1 using "Jobs Segment Object AI" in NIS-Elements software.
Manually refine segmentation masks using the NIS binary editor.
Save corrected images and masks.
AI-2 Training:
Train AI-2 with the corrected datasets using "Jobs Segment Object AI" in NIS-Elements software.
Perform automated quantification using GA3 "8-Count SNc and VTA DA neurons".
Export results as CSV files.
Adjust output directory if needed.
Import data into GraphPad Prism.
Use standard atlas and TH Atlas as references (see document step 11).
Generate curve plots of the number off cell counted for SNc or VTA relative to bregma coordinates.
Compute area under the curve (AUC) per animal.
Record AUC values in dataset: "VTA or SNc TD-Tomato+ cells"
Normalize values relative to WT and KO controls.
Record normalized values in: "VTA or SNc TD-Tomato+ cells (%)"
creator: Amandine Even
